questions.question: To reduce the number of circulator models needed and still cover a wide range of performance requirements, most circulator manufacturers now offer wet rotor circulators capable of operating at ____ different speeds.
If the system experiencing cavitation is operating at a relatively high water temperature (above 190°F), try lowering the boiler aquastat setting by about ____.
The fluid's mechanical energy content is increased as it is accelerated toward the inner edge of the impeller.

One of the Hydraulic Institute's recent undertakings was to develop a __________ that allows for energy use comparisons between circulators under consideration for a specific hydronic system application.
Circulators with 'flat' pump curves should be used when the objective is to maintain a relatively turbulent pressure differential across the distribution system over a wide range of flow rates.
The pump curve for two identical circulators connected in parallel is obtained by doubling the flow rate of a single circulator at each head value.
When designing a piping system, select a circulator that delivers up to ____ more head than required at the desired flow rate.

The ER (Energy Rating) procedure involves gathering several types of performance data for a circulator, including its ____________, along with electrical input power and hydraulic output power at several flow rates.
The rate at which a circulator adds head to the fluid in a piping system equals the rate at which the piping system dissipates this head, and this system is said to be in hydraulic equilibrium.
The motor of a wet rotor circulator is totally cooled and lubricated by the system's fluid.
The circulators used in hydronic systems are technically classified as ____ pumps.
A flow-check valve, or spring-loaded check valve, needs to be installed in each piping circuit anytime multiple circulators are installed ____.

One of the biggest advancements in hydronic heating technology over the past two decades is the development of circulators that can __________________ to adjust to differential pressure changes within the distribution system.
The most common method of connecting a circulator to piping is with bolted flanges. A special type of flange, known as an isolation flange, contains a built-in ____.

The net positive suction head available (NPSHA) is a precise description of the state of the fluid, within a specific piping system, as it enters the circulator. It includes the effects of temperature, pressure, velocity, and the fluid's ____ into a single number.
Every circulator has a minimum required value of net positive suction head in order to operate without cavitation. This value is called the __________________.
Another way of installing circulators in series is called a(n) __________________.
The circulator must first be filled with water and purged of most air before it can operate properly. This is called _______.
Most circulators used in smaller hydronic systems are designed to be installed with their shafts in a horizontal position.
The circulator must first be filled with water and purged of most air before it can operate properly. This is called ____.
Always install the circulator so that its inlet is close to the connection point of the system's expansion tank.
For a liquid at a given temperature, cavitation occurs anywhere the liquid's pressure drops below its vapor pressure.
An increase in pressure from the inlet to outlet port of an operating circulator is the 'evidence' that ____ has been added to the fluid.
When vapor pockets form from liquid water, the density inside the vapor pocket is about ______ lower than the surrounding liquid.
For a liquid at a given temperature, cavitation occurs anywhere the liquid’s pressure ____ its vapor pressure.
